treatment_information.specific_treatment: Cardiac catheterization with drug-eluting stent (DES) placement
treatment_information.dosage_or_details: DES placed in RCA due to 99% stenosis and thrombus
treatment_information.treatment_response: Patient became hypotensive and bradycardic during procedure but responded well to atropine, lidocaine, and amiodarone

treatment_timeline.event: Patient presented with abdominal pain and shortness of breath (SOB)
Performed cardiac catheterization, finding 99% RCA mid stenosis with thrombus
Developed hypotension and bradycardia during procedure
Stabilized on lidocaine drip in CCU

patient_outcome.final_status: Confused but alert and interactive; discharged home
patient_outcome.follow_up_details: Follow up with primary care doctor and cardiologist
patient_outcome.long_term_prognosis: Heart function may recover over time
patient_outcome.patient_perspective: Increased confusion noted during hospital stay, expected to improve after discharge
